triage.clinical_question: What medication adherence, dosing changes, or recent lab follow-up could explain the subtherapeutic valproate level and recent decline?
decision_factors: Alert category 'medication_discrepancy' can indicate gradual deterioration.
Patient identity and timestamp are present; alert source_id rpm:P005:alert:A005 is preserved for traceability.
Valproate level is 34.0 mcg/mL, which is below the supplied low threshold of 50.0 mcg/mL.
Current level is decreased from baseline 68.0 mcg/mL, supporting a meaningful downward change.
Laboratory verification is true, which supports measurement reliability.
Alert category is medication_discrepancy, which the deterministic tool classifies as Urgent.
No repeat measurement, symptom report, or detailed trend data were provided, so clinical context is limited.

relevant_notes.title: Neurology follow-up
relevant_notes.text: No reported seizure activity. Continue current valproate dose and monitor levels.

Any follow-up valproate levels or related labs after 2026-05-10

symptoms_and_timeline: 2026-05-12: No seizure symptoms or new neurologic complaints reported.
2026-05-12: Patient was surprised that the valproate level was low.
medication_adherence: 2026-05-12: Reports taking every valproate dose and denies intentional or accidental missed doses.

patient_concerns: Concerned/surprised that the valproate level was low.

No other potential causes of subtherapeutic valproate level were described in the record.

brief.alert_summary: Urgent medication discrepancy alert for a subtherapeutic valproate level: 34 mcg/mL, below the low threshold of 50 mcg/mL and decreased from a prior baseline of 68 mcg/mL. The measurement was laboratory-verified.
brief.patient_snapshot: 42-year-old female with active epilepsy on valproate 500 mg twice daily. Recent EHR lab on 2026-05-10 also showed valproate 34 mcg/mL (flagged sub-therapeutic).

contextual_analysis.text: RPM alert shows valproate 34 mcg/mL, below the supplied low threshold of 50 mcg/mL, with a lower baseline of 68 mcg/mL.
EHR lists active epilepsy and active valproate therapy at 500 mg twice daily.
EHR lab on 2026-05-10 also recorded valproate 34 mcg/mL with a sub-therapeutic flag, supporting that this is not an isolated portal value.
Neurology follow-up on 2026-02-19 documented no reported seizure activity and advised continuing the current dose while monitoring levels.
Patient reported taking every valproate dose, denied missed doses, and reported no new supplements or major diet changes.
Patient reported no seizure symptoms or new neurologic complaints on 2026-05-12.

risk_assessment.text: Persistently low valproate levels may indicate reduced medication exposure despite reported adherence, which could affect seizure control risk in a patient with epilepsy.
Because the level is already documented as sub-therapeutic in the EHR, the main concern is confirmation of timing, dosing, or other causes of the low value rather than a single unexpected result.
Current absence of symptoms is reassuring, but it does not exclude clinical risk from a sustained low valproate level.

recommended_actions.action: Review medication administration history, refill history, and any dose or formulation changes since the last neurology follow-up.
recommended_actions.rationale: This may explain the low valproate level despite patient-reported adherence.

recommended_actions.action: Verify timing of the blood draw relative to the last valproate dose and confirm whether the 2026-05-10 level was intended as a trough.
recommended_actions.rationale: Timing mismatch can make a level appear lower than expected.

recommended_actions.action: Consider clinician review of whether a repeat valproate level or related follow-up is needed, given the repeated sub-therapeutic result in RPM and EHR.
recommended_actions.rationale: A repeat result can help confirm persistence and trend.

recommended_actions.action: Assess for any new neurologic symptoms or seizure activity during clinician follow-up.
recommended_actions.rationale: Ongoing symptom review helps determine near-term clinical significance.

uncertainties_and_gaps: No documented missed doses, refill history, or medication reconciliation details.
No documented valproate dose changes, formulation changes, or recent hospitalization details after 2026-02-19.
No repeat valproate level after 2026-05-10 is available.
No timing of last dose relative to blood draw is provided.
No explanation for the downward change from 68 to 34 mcg/mL is documented.
No symptoms or seizure recurrence were reported, but this is limited to the available anamnesis.
brief.disclaimer: Educational-prototype clinical synthesis only; not a diagnosis, prescription, or directive. Requires clinician review and local clinical judgment.
